title: File System (fs) Module Quiz
description: Evaluate your understanding of async vs sync reading/writing and the modern fs.promises approach for clean async/await implementations in Node.js.

questions.question: Which and/or what is the primarily design philosophy of the 'fs' module in Node.js?
options: A system focused on interacting with files and folders on the server's disk.
To hide the JavaScript code from the console.

questions.question: Which and/or what was the primarily benefit of using 'fs.readFile' (async) instead of 'fs.readFileSync' (sync)?
options: fs.readFile is non-blocking, so it doesn't stop the rest of your server while it waits for the disk to read the file.

questions.question: Which method in the 'fs' module would you use to add new content to the end of an existing file without deleting its original content?
Does Node.js allow you to use Async/Await to read files in modern versions?
Which and/or what method is used in the 'fs' module to delete a file from the disk?
